grounds.finding: No C1 property is violated by the described behavior. The reviewer checked all relevant properties (P2, P4, P6, P9, P12, P15, P16, P17, P18, P19) and found zero violations. A proposal that does not fix a property violation is not a valid improvement under the autospec contract.

P15 (CEPendingRecoveryBounded) contains the explicit disjunct 'pa.session /= ob_primary[pa.market]' as an escape clause. This is affirmative spec-design evidence that old-session pending fills are expected to persist in archiver stores and be processed by CE. The proposal treats designed behavior as a bug.

The 'phantom CEFinalization' consequence does not hold. CEProcessRecoveredPending has msgs_ce_to_ob in its UNCHANGED clause (spec line 341-345). Recovered pending fills never send to msgs_ce_to_ob and therefore never trigger CEFinalization regardless of session. The proposal's consequence claim is false.

The diff conflicts with the step 3 accepted proposal. Step 3 collapses MFR+Snapshot to a single MFR in CEProcessRecoveredPending. This proposal's replacement of the same action still references MFR+Snapshot pairs. The proposals cannot both apply cleanly.

The fix introduces a real liveness hazard: CEProcessRecoveredPending is blocked on ce_cached_ob_session until CEReceiveOBViewChange fires, creating a message ordering dependency absent from the current spec. Domain fact #5 (archivers fire-and-forget, no cleanup) is consistent with CE processing old-session pending fills without a freshness gate.

ruling.proposer_credit: The 7-step trace is accurate and the behavioral pattern is correctly identified. The proposal is technically coherent. The failure is that the identified behavior is not a defect: it is intentional per P15 and causes no property violation.

next_step_guidance: Do not re-propose a session freshness gate on CEProcessRecoveredPending without first identifying a concrete C1 property violated by old-session fill processing. The P15 escape clause must be addressed in any future proposal on this action.
